data.name: Dread Claws Warlock Endgame Build Guide

skill.name: Dread Claws
skill.description: Burst forth 4 shadowy claws, each dealing {value1} damage.

skill.name: Rampage
skill.description: Summon a rampaging brute for 10 seconds that smashes wildly, dealing {value1} damage with a 5% chance to Eviscerate enemies for {value2} damage.

skill.name: Sigil of Summons
skill.description:
Create a binding ritual for 10 seconds that extends the duration of your Summons.
Killing 8 enemies or Demons within fuels the ritual to summon a demon for 20 seconds which attacks nearby enemies, dealing {value1} damage per hit.
You can have up to 3 demons active at once.

skill.name: Summon Laalish
skill.description: Summon Laalish, the Abyss Demon.

skill.name: Metamorphosis
skill.description:
Transform into a demon and gain:
- Demonform
- All Archfiend Demonform Skill variants
- A special melee Basic attack that deals {value1} damage

All overriden Skills will be at least the same Rank as Metamorphosis.
Cast again while active to cancel.

skill.name: Profane Sentinel
skill.description: Summon a demonic eye for 15 seconds that focuses upon enemies with a beam that makes them Vulnerable and periodically blasts them, dealing {value1} damage. Recast to direct the eye's targeting.

skill.name: Dread Claws
skill.description: Burst forth 4 shadowy claws, each dealing {value1} damage.

skill.name: Rampage
skill.description: Summon a rampaging brute for 10 seconds that smashes wildly, dealing {value1} damage with a 5% chance to Eviscerate enemies for {value2} damage.

skill.name: Sigil of Subversion
skill.description:
Create a subversive ritual for 10 seconds that Hexes enemies.
Killing 8 enemies or Demons within fuels the ritual to send forth shadow trails for 8 seconds that grant you 1 stack of Shadowform every second while in them.
You can have up to 3 trails active at once.

children.text: This Variant is about being as tanky as possible.
On this variant we make use of the fact that
Ritual stays infinitely up at max value (until we logoff or die). We cast any Sigil such as
then swap it off our Bars for
Enough cooldown or Ultimate Duration to permantly use
In terms of stats, I think just stacking
is an insane damage multiplier. It might not be the absolute best for
, but if you just want to play the game, never die, and feel basically immortal while one-shotting everything… it’s still the best choice.
, your best bet is probably farming
, those are the bosses to hit. And if you’re trying to target farm other specific uniques, then
is always the best place to go.

children.text: Build Guide teaches you about Gear, Skills, Paragon, Playstyle & Seasonal Mechanics in Diablo 4 Season 13.
??Follow me for live gameplay, meta testing & updates on my
??Build Showcases and Guide Videos can be found on my
Check out the instructions on how to use my easy to setup
If you are not level 70 yet, make sure to check out the Leveling Guide Tier List.
When it is on Cooldown use
Make sure to cast it once every 9-10 seconds to summon all Shard Demons with
Keep casting it until you have 5 up.
Cast it once every 12 seconds for
twice then Evade to teleport with
when you need to dodge something.
Cast it at least once every 3 seconds.
Grants a massive amount of Damage Reduction with
Generates 4 Shadowform Stacks to use up with
It will cast all other Shard Demons via
and generates a Greater Demon for
Keep casting it until you have 5 up.
Cast it once every 12 seconds for
twice then Evade to teleport with
when you need to dodge something, at least once every 2-3 seconds.
Grants a massive amount of Damage Reduction with
Generates 4 Shadowform Stacks to use up with
Once the Ritual reaches 200% Damage and 40% Damage Reduction it will just stay on until you logout.
Take off the point in the Sigil Skill
because it is much easier to hit and does higher damage. In order to get the most out of it, we need a Greater Demon summon. Our choices depend on our level of progression, but ultimately,
is our choice as it doubles down as a Recast skill for
because it provides a tremendous amount of damage and survivability
5pc, we summon all Shard Demons for 10 seconds and get [x]200% more damage on top of 34% Damage Reduction.
We get a total of 11 Greater Demons:
This amounts to a [x]330% damage bonus from
2pc bonus also grants us 25% Attack Speed and
All in all, this Set provides a ~15x damage multiplier!
is used as a means to proc
which also summons a Greater Demon for
This build is considerably tankier than the norm with our many sources of damage reduction.
This is the perfect build for
, we gain a tremendous amount of Damage Reduction via
consumes Shadowform Stacks meaning we can reset our Evade in 2-3
to grant us 45% Barrier every 3 seconds.
We can stack Barrier Generation on our gear to make this even stronger.
for a massive amount of Damage Reduction against distant enemies.
The final piece of the puzzle is just stacking Maximum Life like on every other build.
We can solve this through a combination of different things:
+ 1x Wrath Regeneration keeps up topped up no matter our Attack Speed.
you will have to play around with the amount of Wrath Regeneration affixes.
How do I Know Which Stat to Focus on?
Because the game's server operates in

(images), Skills can only be cast in a whole number of frames. This means, for example, that you can't cast a Skill in 10.2 frames. To extend this example further, that means that all Attack Speed values that lead to a cast time of 10.01 to 10.99 frames would mean you end up casting at 11 frames, effectively wasting all Attack Speed gains from 11 frames to 10.01.
For that reason, it is important to reach the Attack Speed Breakpoint of a frame.

You might be wondering how frames translate into actual Cast Speed. Because the server runs at 60 frames per second, the way we translate frames into Cast Speed is with the formula: 60 / Frames = Casts per Second.
To find everything you need to know about Masterworking and Greater Affixes make sure to check our
If you have any questions about this guide feel free to reach out to me whilst live on
or contact me offline via my
Updated Endgame, Starter and Mephisto Killer
skill gives a nice increase to critical strike damage, but the real reason I like him here is the extra movement speed you get whenever you damage elite enemies. It’s always welcome.

skill, which feels really good with all the minions running around.
